lesson.menu_desc: Check in, find your gate and make polite requests with Could I...? and Where can I...? -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- At the Airport -- checking in and making polite requests for travel
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rubens Tofolo | Aula 17 -- At the Airport

listenings.text: Good afternoon, passengers. This is a boarding announcement for flight four seven two to Lisbon. We are now boarding at gate twenty-six. Please have your boarding pass and passport ready for the agent. Passengers with a connecting flight should check the screens for their next gate after they land. Only one carry-on bag is allowed in the cabin, and it must fit in the overhead bin. We are sorry to say that boarding is delayed by twenty minutes because of a late aircraft. We will start boarding business class and families with small children first. Thank you for your patience, and have a pleasant flight.

I travel abroad two or three times a year for medical conferences, so the airport is like a second home to me. When I arrive, the first thing I do is check in and drop off my big suitcase. I always keep my laptop and my notes in my carry-on, because I do not want to lose them. After check-in, I go through security, and then I look for my gate. If I have time, I find a quiet place near the gate to read. I have learned that a few polite phrases make everything easier. I always say, could you help me, please? or where can I find the lounge? People are happy to help when you ask nicely. Travel is much less stressful when you stay calm and ask good questions.

extra_audio.text: Here is what I do every time I fly. First, I check in and show my passport at the desk. Then I drop off my suitcase and keep my carry-on with me. After that, I go through security with my boarding pass ready. Next, I find my gate and check the screen for any delay. Finally, I wait near the gate until they call my row to board.
